data.passage:
Unit 17 - 01번: 수정과 편집이 자유로운 비선형 영상 편집 시스템의 장점
① All editing systems are now nonlinear computer-based systems that allow random access to any video shot or scene without having to fast forward or fast reverse to find it.
모든 편집 시스템은 이제 어떤 비디오 숏이나 장면을 찾기 위해 빨리 감기나 빨리 되감기를 할 필요 없이 어떤 비디오 숏이나 장면으로의 임의적 접근을 가능하게 하는 비선형 컴퓨터 기반 시스템이다.
② Nonlinear systems can create a range of special effects, such as slow motion, wipes and dissolves.
비선형 시스템은 슬로 모션, 와이프 그리고 디졸브 같은 다양한 특수 효과를 만들 수 있다.
③ Another highlight of a digital nonlinear system is its random access process that makes it easy for an editor to find desired shots or scenes without having to spend time fast forwarding or rewinding videotape.
디지털 비선형 시스템의 또 다른 주요 특징은 편집자가 비디오테이프를 빨리 감거나 되감는 데 시간을 들일 필요 없이 원하는 숏이나 장면을 찾는 것을 쉽게 해 주는 그것의 임의적 접근 과정이다.
④ With nonlinear editing, shots or scenes can be easily added or removed anywhere in the program, and the computer adjusts the program length automatically.
비선형 편집으로는 숏이나 장면을 프로그램의 어디에나 쉽게 추가하거나 삭제할 수 있으며,
컴퓨터는 프로그램의 길이를 자동으로 조정한다.
⑤ Linear editing was like composing a paper on a typewriter. 선형 편집은 타자기로 글을 작성하는 것과 같았다.
⑥ If a mistake was made or new information needed to be added the whole piece had to be retyped.
만약 실수가 생겼거나 새로운 정보가 추가되어야 할 필요가 있다면, 전체를 다시 작성해야 했다.
⑦ Nonlinear editing, on the other hand, is like using a word processing program.
반면, 비선형 편집은 워드 프로세싱 프로그램을 사용하는 것과 같다.
⑧ If a mistake is made, it is easily deleted and fixed with a few keystrokes, and new information can be added easily.
만약 실수가 생기면 그것은 몇 번의 키 입력으로 쉽게 삭제되고 수정될 수 있으며, 새로운 정보가 쉽게 추가될 수 있다.

data.topic_en: Topic: Abstract feelings influencing spatial behavior in public transport
data.topic_ko: 지하철 승객들의 공간 이용 행태는 단순히 편리함뿐 아니라 눈 맞춤 회피 같은 추상적 감정에도 영향을 받는다. 우리가 볼 수 없는 감정들도 실재하며 우리의 공간 경험을 형성한다.
data.expected_title: Invisible Feelings That Shape Our Use of Space
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 연구 사례 중심 / 추상적 개념 설명
data.mood_change: 객관적 관찰 (monitor surroundings) → 추상적 통찰 (feelings influence experience)
data.passage_html: ① To monitor our surroundings is to focus on what's outside of ourselves: what we see, hear, smell, feel, and perhaps even taste. ② But sometimes what really marks a place is something less specific ─ a feeling within us. ③ An interesting example emerged from a study of subway passenger behavior. ④ Researchers trying to understand why people sit where they sit or stand where they stand in subway and metro trains examined the factors that shape the way riders used and navigated that space in different situations. ⑤ One of their findings involved the reasons many riders like to plant themselves close to the train's doors. ⑥ Partly this was the obvious convenience of being able to exit more quickly. ⑦ But it was shaped partly by a more abstract sensation ─ the desire to avoid the sometimes uncomfortable feeling of accidentally making eye contact with seated passengers. ⑧ We can't see feelings ─ but they're very real, and they influence our experience of the world.
data.topic_sentence_en: We can't see feelings ─ but they're very real, and they influence our experience of the world.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 우리는 감정들을 볼 수 없지만, 그것들은 매우 실재하고 세상에 대한 우리의 경험에 영향을 미친다. 눈에 보이지 않는 추상적 감정도 공간 이용에 실질적 영향을 준다는 핵심 주제.

sentences.sentence_html: To monitor our surroundings is to focus on what's outside of ourselves: what we see, hear, smell, feel, and perhaps even taste.

analysis.phrase: what we see, hear, smell, feel

sentences.translation: 우리 주변을 살피는 것은 / 우리 자신 밖의 것에 집중하는 것 / 보고 듣고 냄새 맡고 느끼는 것

sentences.sentence_html: But sometimes what really marks a place is something less specific ─ a feeling within us. An interesting example emerged from a study of subway passenger behavior.
sentences.translation: 그러나 때로 장소를 진정 특징짓는 것은 / 덜 구체적인 것, 즉 우리 내면의 감정 / 흥미로운 예가 지하철 승객 연구에서 나왔다

sentences.sentence_html: Researchers trying to understand why people sit where they sit or stand where they stand in subway trains examined the factors that shape the way riders used and navigated that space.

analysis.phrase: why people sit where they sit

sentences.translation: 사람들이 왜 그 자리에 앉거나 서는지 이해하려는 연구자들이 / 승객들이 공간을 사용하고 탐색하는 방식을 형성하는 요인들을 조사했다

sentences.sentence_html: One of their findings involved the reasons many riders like to plant themselves close to the train's doors. Partly this was the obvious convenience of being able to exit more quickly.
sentences.translation: 연구 결과 중 하나는 승객들이 출입문 근처에 자리 잡는 이유와 관련됐다 / 부분적으로 더 빨리 내릴 수 있는 명백한 편리함

sentences.sentence_html: But it was shaped partly by a more abstract sensation ─ the desire to avoid the uncomfortable feeling of accidentally making eye contact with seated passengers.
sentences.translation: 그러나 이는 부분적으로 더 추상적 감각에 의해 형성됐다 / 앉은 승객과 우연히 눈 마주침의 불편한 느낌을 피하려는 욕구

sentences.sentence_html: We can't see feelings ─ but they're very real, and they influence our experience of the world.
sentences.translation: 우리는 감정을 볼 수 없지만 / 그것들은 매우 실재하며 / 세상에 대한 우리 경험에 영향을 미친다

blank_inference: 장소를 특징짓는 것이 구체적 요소가 아닌 '추상적 감정'이라는 대조
지하철 출입문 근처 선호 이유가 편리함뿐 아니라 '눈 맞춤 회피'라는 심리적 요인
보이지 않는 감정이 '실재하며 경험에 영향'을 미친다는 결론
빈칸: feelings, abstract sensation, invisible but real factors
transformations.mood_change_desc:
글 전체가 객관적 관찰에서 추상적 통찰로 이동.
①에서 구체적 감각(보고 듣고)으로 시작해 ②에서 'less specific feeling'으로 전환.
⑦에서 추상적 감각(abstract sensation)을 강조하며 ⑧에서 보이지 않지만 실재하는 감정의 영향력을 결론짓는다.
grammar_points: To부정사 주어: 'To monitor ~ is to focus'처럼 주어와 보어가 모두 To부정사
선행사 포함 관계대명사 what: 'what really marks', 'what we see' 등
현재분사구 후치수식: 'Researchers trying to understand' (능동 의미)
수동태: 'was shaped by' (추상 감정에 의해 형성됨)
transformations.title_theme_options:
제목: '보이지 않는 감정의 힘' 또는 '추상적 감정이 만드는 공간 선택'
주제: 공간 이용 행태에 영향을 미치는 추상적·심리적 요인의 실재성

data.background: 환경심리학과 행동과학 연구에서는 사람들의 공간 이용이 물리적 편의성뿐 아니라 사회적 불편함 회피, 개인 공간 확보 욕구 같은 심리적 요인에도 크게 영향받음을 밝혀왔다. 지하철처럼 밀폐된 공공장소에서 눈 맞춤 회피는 대표적 사회심리 현상이다.

data.passage:
Unit 17 - 02번: 공간 이용 방식에 영향을 미치는 사람들의 추상적인 감정
① To monitor our surroundings is to focus on what's outside of ourselves: what we see, hear, smell, feel, and perhaps even taste.
우리 주변 환경을 살피는 것은 우리 자신 바깥에 있는 것, 즉 우리가 보고, 듣고, 냄새 맡고, 느끼고 어쩌면 맛보는 것에도 집중하는 것이다.
② But sometimes what really marks a place is something less specific ─ a feeling within us.
그러나 때로는 어떤 장소를 진정으로 특징짓는 것은 덜 구체적인 것, 즉 우리 내부의 '감정'이다.
③ An interesting example emerged from a study of subway passenger behavior.
흥미로운 예가 지하철 승객의 행동에 관한 연구에서 나왔다.
④ Researchers trying to understand why people sit where they sit or stand where they stand in subway and metro trains examined the factors that shape the way riders used and navigated that space in different situations.
지하철과 전철에서 왜 사람들이 그들이 앉는 곳에 앉거나 그들이 서는 곳에 서는지를 이해하려는 연구자들은 다양한 상황에서 승객들이 그 공간을 사용하고 탐색하는 방식을 형성하는 요인들을 조사했다.
⑤ One of their findings involved the reasons many riders like to plant themselves close to the train's doors.
연구 결과 중 하나는 많은 승객들이 열차의 출입문 근처에 자리 잡기를 좋아하는 이유들과 관련이 있었다.
⑥ Partly this was the obvious convenience of being able to exit more quickly.
부분적으로 이것은 더 빨리 내릴 수 있다는 명확한 편리함 때문이었다.
⑦ But it was shaped partly by a more abstract sensation ─ the desire to avoid the sometimes uncomfortable feeling of accidentally making eye contact with seated passengers.
그러나 이는 부분적으로 더 추상적인 느낌, 즉 앉아 있는 승객들과 우연히 눈이 마주치는 때때로 불편한 느낌을 피하려는 욕구에 의해 형성되었다.
⑧ We can't see feelings ─ but they're very real, and they influence our experience of the world.
우리는 감정들을 볼 수 없지만, 그것들은 매우 실재하고, 그것들은 세상에 대한 우리의 경험에 영향을 미친다.

data.topic_en: The Diving Reflex in Humans as Marine Mammals
data.topic_ko: 인간은 다른 해양 포유류처럼 잠수 반사를 가지고 있다. 얼굴이 물에 잠기면 기도가 닫히고 심박수가 느려지며 혈액이 중요 장기로 이동하여 생명을 보호한다. 이는 육상 영장류에는 없는 해양 포유류의 특징이다.
data.expected_title: The Diving Reflex: Human Adaptation for Underwater Survival
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 과학적 현상 설명 / 대조 구조
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (객관적 설명문)
data.passage_html: We have a 'diving reflex', like other marine mammals. This means that special nerve endings on our faces, around the mouth and nose, trigger this reflex only when the facial region goes under water. If we are in the water, with our head out in the air, there is no diving reflex. But if we sink just our face in a bowl of water, while the whole of the rest of our body is in the dry air, the diving reflex is triggered. It automatically closes down the airway, reducing the risk of swallowing water, and it narrows the small air-passages in the lungs. At the same time the heart rate is slowed down to half speed and blood is shunted to the vital organs, protecting them from the effects of the brief stop in breathing. By contrast, if a chimpanzee or a gorilla found itself in water with its face below the surface, it would panic, its heart would race and it would quickly drown.
data.topic_sentence_en: We have a 'diving reflex', like other marine mammals.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 우리는 다른 해양 포유류처럼 '잠수 반사'를 가지고 있다. 인간이 해양 포유류의 생존 메커니즘을 공유함을 보여주는 핵심 문장.

sentences.sentence_html: We have a 'diving reflex', like other marine mammals.
sentences.translation: 우리는 잠수 반사를 가지고 있다 / 다른 해양 포유류처럼

sentences.pattern_name: 명사절 that + 시간 조건 only when
sentences.sentence_html: This means that special nerve endings on our faces, around the mouth and nose, trigger this reflex only when the facial region goes under water.

analysis.phrase: on our faces, around the mouth and nose
only when the facial region goes under water

sentences.translation: 이것은 의미한다 / 얼굴의 특수 신경 말단이, 입과 코 주변의, / 이 반사를 유발한다는 것을 / 얼굴 부위가 물 아래로 들어갈 때만

sentences.pattern_name: 대조 구문 (If... no / But if... triggered)
sentences.sentence_html: If we are in the water, with our head out in the air, there is no diving reflex. But if we sink just our face in a bowl of water, while the whole of the rest of our body is in the dry air, the diving reflex is triggered.

analysis.phrase: If we are in the water, with our head out
if we sink just our face

sentences.translation: 만약 우리가 물속에 있고 머리는 공기 중에 나와 있으면, / 잠수 반사는 없다. / 하지만 만약 얼굴만 물에 가라앉히고, / 나머지 몸은 마른 공기 중에 있으면, / 잠수 반사가 유발된다

sentences.sentence_html: It automatically closes down the airway, reducing the risk of swallowing water, and it narrows the small air-passages in the lungs.

analysis.phrase: It automatically closes down the airway
reducing the risk of swallowing water

sentences.translation: 그것은 자동으로 기도를 닫아, / 물을 삼킬 위험을 줄이고, / 폐 속의 작은 공기 통로를 좁힌다

sentences.pattern_name: 동시 동작 (At the same time) + 분사구문
sentences.sentence_html: At the same time the heart rate is slowed down to half speed and blood is shunted to the vital organs, protecting them from the effects of the brief stop in breathing.

analysis.phrase: the heart rate is slowed down
blood is shunted to the vital organs

sentences.translation: 동시에 / 심박수가 절반 속도로 느려지고 / 혈액이 중요 장기로 보내져, / 짧은 호흡 정지의 영향으로부터 그것들을 보호한다

sentences.sentence_html: By contrast, if a chimpanzee or a gorilla found itself in water with its face below the surface, it would panic, its heart would race and it would quickly drown.

data.background: 잠수 반사(diving reflex)는 해양 포유류가 물속에서 생존하기 위해 진화시킨 생리 반응으로, 심박수 감소, 기도 폐쇄, 혈액의 중요 장기 집중 등을 포함한다. 인간도 이 반사를 보유하고 있어 수중 환경 적응 능력을 일부 가지고 있다.

data.passage:
Unit 17 - 03번: 물속에서 신체 기능을 조절하여 생명을 보호하는 포유류의 잠수 반사 
① We have a 'diving reflex', like other marine mammals. 우리는 다른 해양 포유류처럼 '잠수 반사'를 가지고 있다.
② This means that special nerve endings on our faces, around the mouth and nose, trigger this reflex only when the facial region goes under water.
이것은 입과 코 주변의 얼굴에 있는 특수 신경 말단이 얼굴 부위가 물 아래에 들어갈 때만 이
반사를 유발한다는 것을 의미한다.
③ If we are in the water, with our head out in the air, there is no diving reflex.
만약 우리가, 물속에 있고 머리는 공기 중에 나와 있으면 잠수 반사는 없다.
④ But if we sink just our face in a bowl of water, while the whole of the rest of our body is in the dry air, the diving reflex is triggered.
하지만 만약 우리가 그릇의 물속에 얼굴만 가라앉히고, 나머지 몸 전체는 물기가 없는 공기
중에 있으면, 잠수 반사가 유발된다.
⑤ It automatically closes down the airway, reducing the risk of swallowing water, and it narrows the small air-passages in the lungs.
이것은 기도를 자동으로 닫아, 물을 삼킬 위험을 줄이고, 폐 속의 작은 공기 통로를 좁힌다.
⑥ At the same time the heart rate is slowed down to half speed and blood is shunted to the vital organs, protecting them from the effects of the brief stop in breathing.
동시에 심박수가 절반 속도로 느려지고 혈액이 중요한 장기들로 보내져, 짧은 호흡 정지로 인한 영향으로부터 그것들을 보호한다.
⑦ By contrast, if a chimpanzee or a gorilla found itself in water with its face below the surface, it would panic, its heart would race and it would quickly drown.
반면, 침팬지나 고릴라가 표면 아래에 얼굴이 있는 상태로 물속에 있다면, 그것은 당황하여, 그것의 심장이 빨리 뛰고 금방 익사할 것

data.topic_en: Psychology as an evidence-based science exploring human behavior and mind
data.topic_ko: 심리학은 의견이나 통념이 아니라 증거에 기반하여 인간의 행동과 심리를 이해하려는 과학 분야이다. 일반적 믿음에서 벗어나 실증적 접근을 통해 인간의 보편적 원리와 문화적 다양성을 모두 탐구한다.
data.expected_title: Psychology: The Science of Understanding Human Nature
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 개념 정의 및 대조 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (중립적 설명)
data.passage_html: The common accounts of human nature that float around in society are generally a mixture of assumptions, tales and sometimes plain silliness. However, psychology is different. It is the branch of science that is devoted to understanding people: how and why we act as we do; why we see things as we do; and how we interact with one another. The key word here is 'science.' Psychologists don't depend on opinions and hearsay, or the generally accepted views of society at the time, or even the considered opinions of deep thinkers. Instead, they look for evidence, to make sure that psychological ideas are firmly based, and not just derived from generally held beliefs or assumptions. In addition to this evidence-based approach, psychology deals with fundamental processes and principles that generate our rich cultural and social diversity, as well as those shared by all human beings. These are what modern psychology is all about.
data.topic_sentence_en: Psychologists don't depend on opinions and hearsay, or the generally accepted views of society at the time, or even the considered opinions of deep thinkers. Instead, they look for evidence.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 심리학자들은 의견이나 소문, 당대의 통념이 아니라 증거를 찾는다는 점에서, 심리학이 실증 과학임을 강조하는 핵심 문장이다.

이 글은 심경 변화가 없는 설명문입니다.
통념(assumptions, tales, silliness)과 과학(evidence-based)을 대조하며
심리학의 실증적 특성을 중립적으로 설명합니다.
grammar_points: 관계대명사 that: 'accounts that float', 'branch that is devoted', 'principles that generate' 등 선행사 수식
동격 콜론(:): 'understanding people: how and why...' 앞 내용을 구체적으로 설명
be devoted to -ing: 'devoted to understanding people' 전치사 to 뒤 동명사
부정 병렬: 'don't depend on A, or B, or even C' 세 가지 비과학적 근거 나열
목적 부정사: 'to make sure that~' 증거를 찾는 목적 표현
what 관계대명사: 'what modern psychology is all about' 선행사 포함, '~인 것'

주제: 심리학은 의견이나 통념이 아니라 증거에 기반하여 인간을 이해하는 과학이다
요지: 심리학은 가정이나 소문이 아닌 실증적 증거를 통해 인간의 보편성과 다양성을 탐구하는 과학 분야이다

data.background: 심리학(psychology)은 19세기 말 실험심리학의 등장으로 철학에서 독립한 경험과학이다. 관찰·실험·통계 등 과학적 방법론을 통해 인간의 행동과 정신 과정을 연구하며, 통념이나 직관이 아닌 증거 기반 접근을 핵심으로 한다.

data.passage:
Unit 17 - 04번: 증거에 기반하여 인간의 행동과 심리를 탐구하는 과학으로서의 심리학
① The common accounts of human nature that float around in society are generally a mixture of assumptions, tales and sometimes plain silliness. However, psychology is different.
사회에 떠도는 인간 본성에 대한 흔한 설명은 일반적으로 가정, 이야기, 그리고 때로는 순전한 어리석음의 혼합이다, 그러나 심리학은 다르다.
② It is the branch of science that is devoted to understanding people: how and why we act as we do; why we see things as we do; and how we interact with one another.
그것은 사람들을 이해하는, 즉 우리가 어떻게 그리고 왜 행동하는 대로 행동하는지, 우리가 왜 보는 대로 사물을 보는지, 그리고 우리가 어떻게 서로 상호 작용을 하는지를 이해하는 데 전념하는 과학 분야이다.
③ The key word here is 'science.' 여기서 핵심어는 '과학'이다.
④ Psychologists don't depend on opinions and hearsay, or the generally accepted views of society at the time, or even the considered opinions of deep thinkers.
심리학자들은 의견과 소문, 혹은 당대의 사회에서 일반적으로 받아들여지는 견해, 혹은 심지어 심오한 사상가들의 숙고된 의견에 의존하지 않는다.
⑤ Instead, they look for evidence, to make sure that psychological ideas are firmly based, and not just derived from generally held beliefs or assumptions.
대신에 그들은 심리학적 개념이 단지 일반적으로 받아들여지는 신념이나 가정에서 도출된 것이 아니라, 확고하게 기반을 두고 있는지 확신하기 위해 증거를 찾는다.
⑥ In addition to this evidence-based approach, psychology deals with fundamental processes and principles that generate our rich cultural and social diversity, as well as those shared by all human beings.
이러한 증거 기반 접근법에 더하여 심리학은 모든 인간에 의해 공유되는 근본적인 과정과 원리뿐만 아니라, 우리의 풍부한 문화적 사회적 다양성을 만들어 내는 것들을 다룬다.
⑦ These are what modern psychology is all about. 이것들은 현대 심리학이 무엇인지 보여 준다.

data.topic_en: Topic: How functional relationships bias visual perception
data.topic_ko: 우리의 시각적 인식은 사물과의 기능적 관계에 기반한 무의식적 위계에 의해 형성되며, 이는 객관적으로 보는 능력을 방해한다. 카메라는 기계적 무관심으로 세상을 있는 그대로 기록하지만, 인간의 눈은 선입견과 우선순위에 따라 대상을 왜곡하여 인식한다.
data.expected_title: Vision Biased by Functional Relationships: Beyond Camera-like Perception
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 원인·결과 구조 / 예시를 통한 논증
data.mood_change: 객관적 설명 유지 (심경 변화 없음)
data.passage_html: Vision is influenced by our preconceptions about reality. In viewing a scene, we establish unconscious hierarchies that reflect our functional relationship to objects and our momentary priorities. For example, when visualizing a hammer in our mind's eye, we tend to 'see' it in profile or at some other 'ready for use' angle. One would probably not visualize a hammer as seen from the top so that the handle is hidden by the hammer's head. The functional relationship we have with objects creates visual expectations that interfere with our ability to see 'like a camera.' The camera, like the human eye, sees only shapes and colors. It documents the world impartially through a lens that is similar to the eye. When we look at them carefully, photographs are often surprising because they don't interpret confusing details but simply serve them up to us with a mechanical indifference. And because of their flatness, photographs often contain areas that appear as unrecognizable colors and shapes.
data.topic_sentence_en: The functional relationship we have with objects creates visual expectations that interfere with our ability to see 'like a camera.'

data.passage:
Unit 18 - 01번: 기능적 관계에 의한 선입견이 시각적 인식을 왜곡하는 현상 
① Vision is influenced by our preconceptions about reality. 시각은 현실에 대한 우리의 선입견에 의해 영향을 받는다.
② In viewing a scene, we establish unconscious hierarchies that reflect our functional relationship to objects and our momentary priorities.
한 장면을 볼 때, 우리는 우리의 사물과의 기능적 관계와 우리의 순간적인 우선순위를 반영하는 무의식적인 위계를 확립한다.
③ For example, when visualizing a hammer in our mind's eye, we tend to "see" it in profile or at some other "ready for use" angle.
예를 들어, 우리 마음의 눈으로 망치를 시각화할 때, 우리는 망치를 옆모습이나 '사용 준비 완료' 각도에서 '보는' 경향이 있다.
④ One would probably not visualize a hammer as seen from the top so that the handle is hidden by the hammer's head.
손잡이가 망치 머리에 가려지도록 망치가 위에서 보여진 모습으로 아마 시각화하지 않을 것이다.
⑤ The functional relationship we have with objects creates visual expectations that interfere with our ability to see "like a camera."
우리가 가진 사물과의 기능적인 관계는 '카메라처럼' 보는 우리의 능력을 방해하는 시각적 기대를 만든다.
⑥ The camera, like the human eye, sees only shapes and colors. 카메라는 인간의 눈처럼 오직 형태와 색깔만을 본다.
⑦ It documents the world impartially through a lens that is similar to the eye.
그것은 눈과 비슷한 렌즈를 통해 세상을 공평하게 기록한다.
⑧ When we look at them carefully, photographs are often surprising because they don't interpret confusing details but simply serve them up to us with a mechanical indifference.
우리가 사진들을 주의 깊게 들여다볼 때, 그것들은 혼란을 주는 세부 사항들을 해석하는 것이 아니라 기계적인 무관심으로 우리에게 그것들을 단순히 제공해 주기 때문에 흔히 놀랍다.
⑨ And because of their flatness, photographs often contain areas that appear as unrecognizable colors and shapes.
그리고 그것들의 평면성 때문에 사진들은 흔히 알아보기 어려운 색과 형태들로 보이는 영역을
포함한다.
⑩ [Summary] Our visual perception is shaped by an established hierarchy based on functional relationships, which interrupts our ability to see objects as they truly are, unlike the objective perspective of a camera.
[Summary] 우리의 시각적 인식은 기능적 관계에 기반한 확립된 위계에 의해 형성되며, 이는 카메라의 객관적인 시각과는 달리 사물을 있는 그대로 보는 능력을 방해한다.

data.topic_en: Topic: The Necessity of Creative Thinking Beyond Linear Thinking for Solving New Problems
data.topic_ko: 새로운 문제 해결에는 논리적·선형적 사고만으로는 부족하며, 창의적·비선형적 사고가 필요하다. 익숙한 영역에서는 논리적 사고가 효과적이지만, 혁신적 해결책을 위해서는 창의적 뇌를 활용해야 한다.
data.expected_title: The Limits of Linear Thinking: Why Creativity Matters in Problem-Solving
data.text_type: 논설문 (argumentative) — 대조·비교 구조 / 선형적 사고 vs 창의적 사고
data.mood_change: 논설문으로 심경 변화 없음 — 논리적 주장 전개 (logical thinking의 한계 → creative thinking의 필요성)
data.passage_html: In the course of trying to solve a problem with an invention, you may encounter a brick wall of resistance when you try to think your way logically through the problem. Such logical thinking is a linear type of process, which uses our reasoning skills. This works fine when we're operating in the area of what we know or have experienced. However, when we need to deal with new information, ideas, and viewpoints, linear thinking will often come up short. On the other hand, creativity by definition involves the application of new information to old problems and the conception of new viewpoints and ideas. For this you will be most effective if you learn to operate in a nonlinear manner; that is, use your creative brain. Stated differently, if you think in a linear manner, you'll tend to be conservative and keep coming up with techniques which are already known. This, of course, is just what you don't want.
data.topic_sentence_en: However, when we need to deal with new information, ideas, and viewpoints, linear thinking will often come up short. On the other hand, creativity by definition involves the application of new information to old problems and the conception of new viewpoints and ideas.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 그러나 새로운 정보, 아이디어, 관점을 다뤄야 할 때 선형적 사고로는 흔히 충분하지 않다. 반면 창의성은 정의상 기존 문제에 대한 새로운 정보의 적용과 새로운 관점의 구상을 포함한다. — 논리적 사고의 한계와 창의적 사고의 필요성을 대조하는 핵심 주장.
data.mood_keyword: 대조 논증 (선형적 사고의 한계 vs 창의적 사고의 필요성)

blank_inference: ④번 문장 'linear thinking will often come up ____' → short (부족함을 나타내는 표현)
⑤번 문장 'creativity ____ definition involves' → by (정의상이라는 의미)
⑥번 문장 'operate in a ____ manner' → nonlinear (선형적 vs 비선형적 대조)
⑦번 문장 'you'll ____ to be conservative' → tend (경향성을 나타내는 동사)
transformations.mood_change_desc:
논설문으로 심경 변화는 없으며, 논리적 주장이 단계적으로 전개됨.
① 문제 상황 제시 (논리적 사고의 한계) → ③ 긍정적 측면 인정 → ④ 전환·한계 지적 (However) → ⑤ 대안 제시 (창의적 사고) → ⑥-⑦ 결론 및 경고
grammar_points: 관계대명사 'which' 계속적 용법: 'a linear type of process, which uses'는 추가 설명
관계대명사 'what' 선행사 포함: 'what we know' = 'the thing(s) which we know'
come up short: '부족하다'는 관용 표현, come up with와 구별
분사구문 'Stated differently': '다르게 말하면'이라는 수동 분사구문
tend to: '~하는 경향이 있다'는 습관·성향 표현
강조 구문 'just what': 'what' 앞에 'just'로 '바로 ~인 것' 강조
transformations.title_theme_options: 제목: '창의적 사고의 필요성' 또는 '선형적 사고를 넘어서' / 주제: 새로운 문제 해결에는 논리적 사고의 한계를 넘어 창의적·비선형적 사고가 필요하다

data.background: 문제 해결 과정에서 논리적·선형적 사고(linear thinking)는 익숙한 영역에서는 효과적이지만, 새로운 정보나 관점을 다룰 때는 한계가 있다. 혁신적 해결책을 위해서는 창의적·비선형적 사고(creative, nonlinear thinking)가 필수적이다.

data.passage:
Unit 18 - 02번: 새로운 문제 해결을 위해 선형적 사고를 넘어선 창의적 사고의 필요성
① In the course of trying to solve a problem with an invention, you may encounter a brick wall of resistance when you try to think your way logically through the problem.
어떤 발명품이 가진 문제를 해결하려고 하는 과정에서, 여러분이 문제를 논리적으로 생각해
나가려고 애쓸 때 저항이라는 벽돌 벽에 맞닥뜨릴지도 모른다.
② Such logical thinking is a linear type of process, which uses our reasoning skills.
그러한 논리적 사고는 선형적 과정으로, 우리의 추론 능력을 활용한다.
③ This works fine when we're operating in the area of what we know or have experienced.
이는 우리가 알고 있거나 경험해 본 영역에서 작업할 때는 잘 작동한다.
④ However, when we need to deal with new information, ideas, and viewpoints, linear thinking will often come up short.
그러나 우리가 새로운 정보, 아이디어, 관점을 다뤄야 할 때 선형적 사고로는 흔히 충분하지 않을 것이다.
⑤ On the other hand, creativity by definition involves the application of new information to old problems and the conception of new viewpoints and ideas.
반면, 창의성은 정의상 기존 문제에 대한 새로운 정보의 적용과 새로운 관점과 아이디어의 구상을 포함한다.
⑥ For this you will be most effective if you learn to operate in a nonlinear manner; that is, use your creative brain.
이를 위해서 여러분이 비선형적 방식으로 작업하는 법, 즉 창의적인 뇌를 사용하는 법을 배운다면 여러분은 가장 효과적이 될 것이다.
⑦ Stated differently, if you think in a linear manner, you'll tend to be conservative and keep coming up with techniques which are already known.
다시 말해, 여러분이 선형적인 방식으로 사고하면, 보수적으로 되고 이미 알려진 기술을 계속 떠올리게 될 것이다.
⑧ This, of course, is just what you don't want. 이것이 물론 여러분이 원하지 않는 바로 그것이다.
⑨ [Summary] Logical thinking works well with familiar problems but falls short in dealing with new ideas, for which creative thinking is needed to come up with innovative solutions.
[Summary] 논리적 사고는 익숙한 문제에서는 잘 작동하지만, 새로운 아이디어를 다루는 데에는 불충분한데, 이를 위해서는 창의적 사고가 혁신적인 해결책을 생각해 내는 데에 필요하다

data.topic_en: Topic: Avoiding trick-punishments by rewarding positive behaviors
data.topic_ko: 신경심리학자 Alvaro Bilbao가 제시한 '트릭 처벌' 개념을 통해, 부정적 행동에 관심을 주는 처벌이 오히려 그 행동을 강화할 수 있음을 설명한다. 부모는 부정적 측면을 지적하는 대신 긍정적 행동을 보상하는 전략을 채택해야 한다.
data.expected_title: The Paradox of Trick-Punishments: Rewarding Positives Over Punishing Negatives
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 개념 소개 및 사례 분석 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (개념 설명 중심의 논리적 전개)
data.passage_html: Punishing a child may not be effective due to what Alvaro Bilbao, a neuropsychologist, calls 'trick-punishments.' A trick-punishment is a scolding, a moment of anger or a punishment in the most classic sense of the word. Instead of discouraging the child from doing something, it encourages them to do it. For example, Hugh learns that when he hits his little brother, his mother scolds him. For a child who feels lonely, being scolded is much better than feeling invisible, so he will continue to hit his brother. In this case, his mother would be better adopting a different strategy. For instance, she could congratulate Hugh when he has not hit his brother for a certain length of time. The mother clearly cannot allow the child to hit his little brother, but instead of constantly pointing out the negatives, she can choose to reward the positives. In this way, any parent can avoid trick-punishments. A trick-punishment reinforces the unwanted behavior of a child, which implies that parents should focus on reducing the attention to negatives while rewarding positive behaviors.
data.topic_sentence_en: A trick-punishment reinforces the unwanted behavior of a child, which implies that parents should focus on reducing the attention to negatives while rewarding positive behaviors.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 트릭 처벌은 아이의 바람직하지 못한 행동을 강화하며, 이는 부모가 부정적 측면에 대한 관심을 줄이고 긍정적 행동을 보상하는 데 집중해야 함을 시사한다.

① 문제 제기 (트릭 처벌 개념 소개)

grammar_points: 동명사 주어: 'Punishing a child'처럼 동명사구가 주어로 사용됨
대조 구문: 'Instead of A, B' 형태로 두 가지를 대조
비교급 강조: 'much better than'에서 much가 비교급 강조
관계대명사 계속적 용법: 'which implies that'에서 which가 앞 절 전체를 받음
분사구문: 'while rewarding'에서 접속사 + 분사 형태
조동사 may: 'may not be'로 가능성의 부정 표현

data.passage:
Unit 18 - 03번: 부정적 행동에 대한 관심을 줄이고 긍정적 행동을 보상하는 육아법
① Punishing a child may not be effective due to what Alvaro Bilbao, a neuropsychologist, calls 'trick-punishments.' 아이를 벌주는 것은 신경심리학자 Alvaro Bilbao가 '트릭 처벌'이라고 부르는 것으로 인해 효과적이지 않을 수 있다.
② A trick-punishment is a scolding, a moment of anger or a punishment in the most classic sense of the word.
트릭 처벌은 꾸짖음, 순간의 화 혹은 (처벌이라는) 단어의 가장 전형적인 의미에서의 처벌이다.
③ Instead of discouraging the child from doing something, it encourages them to do it.
아이가 무언가를 하는 것을 단념시키는 대신 트릭 처벌은 그들이 그것을 하도록 장려한다.
④ For example, Hugh learns that when he hits his little brother, his mother scolds him.
예를 들어 Hugh는 그가 자신의 남동생을 때릴 때 그의 어머니가 그를 꾸짖는다는 것을 배운다.
⑤ For a child who feels lonely, being scolded is much better than feeling invisible, so he will continue to hit his brother.
외로움을 느끼는 아이에게는 꾸중을 듣는 것이 눈에 띄지 않는다고 느끼는 것보다 훨씬 나아서 그는 자신의 남동생을 때리는 것을 계속할 것이다.
⑥ In this case, his mother would be better adopting a different strategy. 이 경우에, 그의 어머니는 다른 전략을 채택하는 것이 더 나을 것이다.
⑦ For instance, she could congratulate Hugh when he has not hit his brother for a certain length of time.
예를 들어 그녀는 Hugh가 그의 남동생을 일정 기간 동안 때리지 않았을 때 그를 자랑스러워할 수 있다.
⑧ The mother clearly cannot allow the child to hit his little brother, but instead of constantly pointing out the negatives, she can choose to reward the positives.
어머니는 분명 아이가 그의 남동생을 때리도록 내버려둘 수 없고, 그녀는 부정적 측면을 계속
지적하는 대신에 긍정적 측면을 보상하는 것을 선택할 수 있다.
⑨ In this way, any parent can avoid trick-punishments. 이렇게 어느 부모도 트릭 처벌을 피할 수 있다.
⑩ [Summary] A trick-punishment reinforces the unwanted behavior of a child, which implies that parents should focus on reducing the attention to negatives while rewarding positive behaviors.
[Summary] 트릭 처벌이 아이의 바람직하지 못한 행동을 강화하는데, 이는 부모가 긍정적인 행동을 보상하면서 부정적 측면에 관한 관심을 줄이는 데 집중해야 한다는 것을 시사한다.

data.topic_en: Topic: Capuchin monkeys' sense of fairness and protest behavior against unequal rewards
data.topic_ko: Capuchin 원숭이는 동일한 노력에 대해 다른 보상을 받는 상황을 불공정하게 인식한다. 자신의 절대적 보상은 변하지 않았지만 상대적 불평등을 감지하면 보상을 거부하며 불만을 표현한다.
data.expected_title: Monkeys React to Unfair Treatment
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 실험 사례 중심 / 동물 행동 분석
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (실험 관찰 기록)
data.passage_html: Capuchins ─ New World Monkeys that live in large social groups ─ will, in captivity, trade with people all day long, especially if food is involved. I give you this rock and you give me a treat to eat. If you put two monkeys in cages next to each other, and offer them both slices of cucumber for the rocks they already have, they will happily eat the cucumbers. If, however, you give one monkey grapes instead ─ grapes being universally preferred to cucumbers ─ the monkey that is still receiving cucumbers will begin to throw them back at the experimenter. Even though she is still getting 'paid' the same amount for her effort of sourcing rocks, and so her particular situation has not changed, the comparison to another makes the situation unfair. Furthermore, she is now willing to abandon all gains ─ the cucumbers themselves ─ to communicate her displeasure to the experimenter.
data.topic_sentence_en: Even though she is still getting 'paid' the same amount for her effort of sourcing rocks, and so her particular situation has not changed, the comparison to another makes the situation unfair.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 비록 그녀가 돌을 모은 노력에 대해 같은 양을 여전히 '받고' 있어서 그녀의 특정 상황이 변하지 않았지만, 다른 원숭이와의 비교가 그 상황을 부당하게 만든다. 절대적 보상이 아닌 상대적 비교가 공정성 판단의 기준임을 보여준다.

vocabulary.note: trade A for B: A를 B와 교환하다
prefer A to B: B보다 A를 선호하다

blank_inference: 빈칸: 'the comparison to another makes the situation _____' → unfair (상대적 비교가 공정성 판단 기준)
빈칸: 원숭이는 절대적 보상이 아닌 _____ 보상에 반응한다 → relative/comparative
빈칸: 오이를 던지는 행동은 _____ 의 표현이다 → protest/displeasure
transformations.mood_change_desc:
실험 관찰 기록이므로 심경 변화 없음.
원숭이의 행동 변화: 만족(happily eat) → 항의(throw back)
grammar_points: 동격 dash 용법: 'Capuchins ─ New World Monkeys that...' (삽입 설명)
독립분사구문: 'grapes being universally preferred' (주절과 주어가 다름)
양보절 Even though + 주제문 구조 (절대적 상황은 불변이지만 상대적 비교로 인식 변화)
be willing to 동사원형: 기꺼이 ~하다 (자발적 의지)
transformations.title_theme_options:
제목: '원숭이도 아는 공정성' 또는 '상대적 불평등에 대한 반응'
주제: 동물도 절대적 이익보다 상대적 공정성을 중시한다

data.background: Capuchin 원숭이는 사회적 동물로 공정성에 대한 감각을 지닌다. 이 실험은 동물도 상대적 비교를 통해 불평등을 인식하며, 절대적 보상이 변하지 않아도 타자와의 비교로 부당함을 느끼면 항의 행동을 보임을 증명한다.

data.passage:
Unit 18 - 04번: 불공정한 보상에 대해 불쾌감을 표현하는 원숭이의 행동
① Capuchins ─ New World Monkeys that live in large social groups ─ will, in captivity, trade with people all day long, especially if food is involved.
대규모의 사회 집단으로 서식하는 New World Monkey인 Capuchin은 갇힌 상태에서 온종일
사람들과 거래를 할 것인데 특히 먹이가 연관된다면 그러할 것이다.
② I give you this rock and you give me a treat to eat. '내가 너에게 이 돌을 주고 너는 나에게 먹을 간식을 준다.'
③ If you put two monkeys in cages next to each other, and offer them both slices of cucumber for the rocks they already have, they will happily eat the cucumbers.
만약 당신이 두 마리의 원숭이들을 나란히 있는 우리에 넣고 그들이 이미 가지고 있는 돌의 대가로 오이 조각을 둘 모두에게 주었을 때, 그들은 그 오이를 기쁘게 먹을 것이다.
④ If, however, you give one monkey grapes instead ─ grapes being universally preferred to cucumbers ─ the monkey that is still receiving cucumbers will begin to throw them back at the experimenter.
하지만 만약 당신이 한 원숭이에게는 포도를 대신 준다면, 일반적으로 포도는 오이보다 더 선호되는데, 여전히 오이를 받은 원숭이는 그것들을 실험자에게 던지기 시작할 것이다.
⑤ Even though she is still getting "paid" the same amount for her effort of sourcing rocks, and so her particular situation has not changed, the comparison to another makes the situation unfair.
비록 그녀가 돌을 모은 그녀의 수고에 대한 대가로 같은 양을 여전히 '받고', 그래서 그녀의 특정한 상황이 변화가 없더라도, 다른 원숭이와의 비교는 그 상황을 부당하게 만든다.
⑥ Furthermore, she is now willing to abandon all gains ─ the cucumbers themselves ─ to communicate her displeasure to the experimenter.
게다가, 그녀는 실험자에게 그녀의 불쾌함을 전달하기 위해 모든 얻은 것들, 즉 오이 자체를 이제 기꺼이 포기한다.
⑦ [Summary] According to the passage, if the Capuchin monkey realizes the inequality in rewards compared to another monkey, she will reject her rewards to express her feelings about the treatment, despite getting exactly the same rewards as before.
[Summary] 이 글에 따르면, 만약 Capuchin 원숭이가 다른 원숭이와 비교하여 보상에서의
불평등을 알아차린다면, 그녀는 이전과 정확히 똑같은 보상을 받더라도 대우에 대한 그녀의 감정을 표현하기 위해 그녀의 보상을 거부할 것이다

data.topic_en: Topic: Announcement of free upcycling classes at a second-hand clothing store
data.topic_ko: 중고 의류 매장 Used Threads가 고객들에게 무료 업사이클링 수업을 제공한다는 안내문. 매주 토요일 오후 3~4시에 재봉과 염색 기법으로 헌 옷을 변형하는 방법을 가르친다.
data.expected_title: Invitation to Free Weekly Upcycling Classes
data.text_type: 안내문 (announcement) — 서비스 소개 및 참여 방법 안내
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (informative tone, 일관된 친절함)
data.passage_html:
Dear Valued Customers,
Here at Used Threads, we have always prided ourselves on offering guests a wide selection of second-hand items. By purchasing recycled clothes, customers not only gain unique pieces, but also help to save the environment. Now, we'd like to go a step further, which is why we are launching weekly classes about how to upcycle clothing. These classes will be free and will take place from 3 to 4 p.m. on Saturdays. During the sessions, we will teach customers how to transform their old clothes with techniques like sewing and dyeing. If you're interested in attending a class, please e-mail me at least a day beforehand as spots are limited. We hope to see you there!
Best Regards,
Ari Kim, Used Threads Manager
data.topic_sentence_en: Now, we'd like to go a step further, which is why we are launching weekly classes about how to upcycle clothing.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 이제 우리는 한 걸음 더 나아가고자 하며, 이것이 바로 의류를 업사이클하는 방법에 관한 주간 수업을 시작하는 이유입니다. (매장의 새로운 서비스 제공 목적을 명확히 밝힌 중심문)

analysis.explanation: teach A B 구조로 A에게 B를 가르치다

vocabulary.note: take it to the next level

blank_inference: 빈칸: 'go a step further' 다음에 올 구체적 행동 → 'launching weekly classes about upcycling'
빈칸: 'not only A but also B' 구조에서 B 자리 → 'help to save the environment'
빈칸: 'at least ___ beforehand' → 'a day' (신청 마감 기한)
transformations.mood_change_desc:
이 글은 심경 변화가 없는 안내문입니다.
일관되게 친절하고 격려하는 tone(inviting & informative)을 유지하며,
고객에게 새로운 서비스(업사이클링 수업)를 소개하고 참여를 독려합니다.
grammar_points: 'have prided ourselves on ~ing' 현재완료로 과거부터 현재까지의 지속적 자부심 표현
'not only A but also B' 병렬 구조, 동사 gain과 help가 대등하게 연결
'which is why ~' 관계대명사 계속적 용법으로 앞 문장 전체를 선행사로 받음
'how to + 동사원형' 의문사+to부정사로 명사절(간접의문문) 역할
transformations.title_theme_options:
제목: 'Invitation to Free Upcycling Classes' 또는 '환경 보호를 위한 의류 재활용 수업 안내'
주제: 중고 의류 매장이 고객 대상으로 무료 업사이클링 교육 프로그램을 시작한다는 안내

data.background: 업사이클링(upcycling)은 재활용품을 단순히 재사용하는 수준을 넘어 디자인과 기능을 더해 더 높은 가치의 제품으로 재탄생시키는 친환경 활동입니다. 의류 업계에서는 재봉, 염색, 패치워크 등의 기법으로 헌 옷을 독창적인 패션 아이템으로 변형합니다.

data.topic_en: A woman's desperate attempt to escape from danger in a dark house
data.topic_ko: 어둠 속에서 탈출구를 찾으려 하지만 결국 도망칠 수밖에 없는 Sarah의 극한 긴장과 공포
data.expected_title: Escape in the Darkness
data.text_type: 서사문 (narrative) — 시간 순서 / 인물의 심리·행동 중심
data.mood_change: 긴장·불안 (her muscles tensed up) → 공포·절망 (little else she could do)
data.passage_html: Sarah quietly made her way through the house, her eyes trying to adjust to the darkness that surrounded her. Searching for an escape route or something she could use to defend herself, she could feel her heartbeat hammering in her chest. With every small sound, her muscles tensed up. Sweat poured down her back. Sarah thought she could finally see the front door, but gasped when she realized that a figure stood in front of it. Although his features weren't clear, Sarah knew who it was, and it made her tremble. The person didn't make a sound, but suddenly moved towards her. Screaming for help, Sarah turned to run and sprinted down the hallway. She knew there was little else she could do now.
data.topic_sentence_en: She knew there was little else she could do now.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 이제 그녀가 할 수 있는 다른 일은 거의 없다는 것을 알았다 — 절망적 상황 인식

sentences.sentence_html: Sarah quietly made her way through the house, her eyes trying to adjust to the darkness that surrounded her.
sentences.translation: Sarah는 조용히 집 안을 나아갔다 / 그녀의 눈은 그녀를 둘러싼 어둠에 적응하려 애쓰면서

sentences.sentence_html: Searching for an escape route or something she could use to defend herself, she could feel her heartbeat hammering in her chest.

sentences.sentence_html: Sarah thought she could finally see the front door, but gasped when she realized that a figure stood in front of it.

data.passage:
Sarah quietly made her way through the house, her
eyes trying to adjust to the darkness that surrounded
her. Searching for an escape route or something she
could use to defend herself, she could feel her heartbeat
hammering in her chest. With every small sound, her
muscles tensed up. Sweat poured down her back. Sarah
thought she could finally see the front door, but gasped
when she realized that a figure stood in front of it.
Although his features weren’t clear, Sarah knew who it
was, and it made her tremble. The person didn’t make a
sound, but suddenly moved towards her. Screaming for
help, Sarah turned to run and sprinted down the hallway.
She knew there was little else she could do now.

data.topic_en: Topic: The importance of fact-based perspectives over instinctive biases
data.topic_ko: 많은 사람들이 사회가 악화되고 있다고 생각하지만, 이는 공포스러운 사건에 집중하는 인간의 본능 때문이다. 사실에 근거한 정확한 관점을 개발하는 것이 중요하며, 실제로 살인율 같은 지표는 과거보다 개선되었다.
data.expected_title: Developing Fact-Based Perspectives: Beyond Instinctive Fears
data.text_type: 논설문 (argumentative) — 주장·근거·반례 제시 중심
data.mood_change: 우려·비관 (crisis, deteriorating) → 객관·성찰 (ask based on accurate information)
data.passage_html: When considering the current state of society, many individuals think that conditions are deteriorating and we are facing a crisis. But is this really true? People need to develop a more accurate perspective by relying solely on facts to form their opinions. Of course, it is a natural human instinct to focus on frightening events. This is why people usually remember news stories about serious crimes such as murders much more easily than other types of reports. But this internal filter can easily create the impression that violent acts are occurring with increasing frequency. The truth of the matter is that they are less common now than in the past; in fact, the murder rate in the United States has declined by almost 50 percent over the past 30 years. So, it is important to always ask, 'Are my beliefs based on accurate information?'
data.topic_sentence_en: People need to develop a more accurate perspective by relying solely on facts to form their opinions.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 사람들은 오직 사실에만 의존하여 의견을 형성함으로써 더 정확한 관점을 개발해야 한다. 이는 본능적 편향을 넘어 객관적 사고의 필요성을 강조하는 핵심 주장이다.

sentences.sentence_html: This is why people usually remember news stories about serious crimes such as murders much more easily than other types of reports.

analysis.explanation: '~보다 훨씬 더 쉽게', much가 비교급 강조

sentences.translation: 이것이 사람들이 보통 살인 같은 심각한 범죄에 관한 뉴스를 / 다른 유형의 보도보다 훨씬 더 쉽게 기억하는 이유다

sentences.sentence_html: But this internal filter can easily create the impression that violent acts are occurring with increasing frequency.

sentences.translation: 하지만 이 내적 필터는 쉽게 인상을 만들어낼 수 있다 / 폭력 행위가 증가하는 빈도로 발생하고 있다는

sentences.sentence_html: The truth of the matter is that they are less common now than in the past; in fact, the murder rate in the United States has declined by almost 50 percent over the past 30 years.
sentences.translation: 문제의 진실은 그것들이 과거보다 지금 덜 흔하다는 것이다 / 실제로 미국의 살인율은 지난 30년간 거의 50퍼센트 감소했다

blank_inference: 빈칸: 'develop a more _______ perspective' → accurate (사실 기반의 정확한 관점)
빈칸: 'this internal _______' → filter (편향된 인식을 만드는 내적 필터)
빈칸: 'based on _______ information' → accurate (정확한 정보에 근거)
transformations.mood_change_desc:
초반 '사회가 악화되고 위기'라는 우려·비관에서 시작
중반 '인간 본능의 편향'을 설명하며 객관적 분석 단계로 전환
후반 '실제 통계는 개선'이라는 사실 제시 후 성찰·질문으로 마무리
grammar_points: 분사구문: 'When considering ~'은 접속사+분사 형태로 시간·조건 표현
가주어 it: 'it is ~ to...' 구문에서 to부정사가 진주어
비교급 강조: 'much more easily'에서 much가 비교급 앞 강조 부사
세미콜론 용법: 'are less common; in fact'처럼 관련 문장 연결 및 추가 설명
transformations.title_theme_options:
제목: '사실 기반 관점의 중요성' 또는 '본능을 넘어선 객관적 사고'
주제: 인간의 본능적 편향이 부정적 인식을 만들 수 있으므로, 사실에 근거한 정확한 관점을 개발해야 한다

data.background: 현대 사회에서 미디어 노출과 인지 편향(cognitive bias)은 실제보다 부정적인 인식을 만든다. 특히 '가용성 휴리스틱(availability heuristic)'은 기억하기 쉬운 사건을 과대평가하게 만들며, 통계적 사실과 체감의 괴리를 낳는다.

data.topic_en: Topic: Anthropologists studying their own cultures to gain new insights
data.topic_ko: 인류학자들이 전통적으로 타문화를 연구했으나, 최근에는 자신의 문화를 중립적으로 관찰하는 방식으로 전환하고 있다. Gillian Tett는 이 기법을 은행 부문에 적용해 2008 금융위기를 예측했다.
data.expected_title: Applying Anthropology to One's Own Culture
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 개념 소개 및 사례 제시 중심
data.mood_change: 없음 (중립적 설명)
data.passage_html: In recent years, a number of anthropologists have decided to flip the lens. Throughout much of the history of this field, researchers from Western countries focused on the study of cultures that were beyond their own experiences. Being an 'objective' outsider was thought to be the best way to draw accurate conclusions about the society being observed. Now, however, some researchers have begun to utilize anthropological techniques to increase their knowledge of the societies that they themselves are a part of. Noted anthropologist Gillian Tett decided to apply the practices of this discipline to better understand corporations — she used the framework of anthropology to immerse herself in the banking sector and came to the conclusion that 'risks were building in this strange, shadowy world.' Because of her willingness to view an aspect of her own culture with the same level of neutrality that anthropologists traditionally strive for when studying more 'exotic' ones, Tett was one of the few people to accurately predict the 2008 financial crisis.
data.topic_sentence_en: Because of her willingness to view an aspect of her own culture with the same level of neutrality that anthropologists traditionally strive for when studying more 'exotic' ones, Tett was one of the few people to accurately predict the 2008 financial crisis.

sentences.pattern_name: 동명사 주어 + be thought to V (수동태)
sentences.sentence_html: Being an 'objective' outsider was thought to be the best way to draw accurate conclusions about the society being observed.
sentences.translation: 객관적 외부인이 되는 것이 / 관찰되는 사회에 대해 정확한 결론을 도출하는 최선의 방법이라고 여겨졌다

sentences.sentence_html: Now, however, some researchers have begun to utilize anthropological techniques to increase their knowledge of the societies that they themselves are a part of.
sentences.translation: 그러나 이제 / 일부 연구자들은 자신들이 속한 사회에 대한 지식을 늘리기 위해 인류학 기법을 활용하기 시작했다

sentences.sentence_html: Noted anthropologist Gillian Tett decided to apply the practices of this discipline to better understand corporations — she used the framework of anthropology to immerse herself in the banking sector and came to the conclusion that 'risks were building in this strange, shadowy world.'
sentences.translation: 저명한 인류학자 Gillian Tett는 기업을 더 잘 이해하기 위해 이 학문의 관행을 적용하기로 결정했다 — 그녀는 인류학의 틀을 사용해 은행 부문에 몰입했고 / '이 이상하고 음침한 세계에서 위험이 쌓이고 있다'는 결론에 도달했다

sentences.sentence_html: Because of her willingness to view an aspect of her own culture with the same level of neutrality that anthropologists traditionally strive for when studying more 'exotic' ones, Tett was one of the few people to accurately predict the 2008 financial crisis.
sentences.translation: 자신의 문화의 한 측면을 / 인류학자들이 전통적으로 더 이국적인 문화를 연구할 때 추구하는 것과 같은 수준의 중립성으로 바라보려는 의지 때문에 / Tett는 2008 금융위기를 정확히 예측한 소수 중 한 명이었다

blank_inference: 빈칸: 자신의 문화를 _______ 관점으로 바라본 덕분에 예측 가능 → 'neutrality'(중립성)
빈칸: 인류학자들이 최근 _______ 하기로 결정 → 'flip the lens'(관점 전환)
빈칸: Tett는 은행 부문에 _______ → 'immerse herself'(몰입)
transformations.mood_change_desc:
이 지문은 심경 변화가 아닌 설명문으로, 인류학 연구 방법론의 변화를 중립적으로 설명한다.
전통적 방법(타문화 연구) → 새로운 접근(자문화 연구) → 성공 사례(금융위기 예측)의 논리적 흐름.
grammar_points: 현재완료(have decided, have begun): 최근 완료된 변화 강조
동명사 주어(Being an outsider): 추상 개념을 주어로
관계대명사 that: cultures/neutrality 수식
to부정사의 다양한 용법: 목적(to increase), 형용사적(to predict), 부사적(to better understand)
transformations.title_theme_options:
제목: '자문화 연구의 새로운 가능성' 또는 '인류학적 관점의 전환'
주제: 인류학자들이 자신의 문화를 객관적으로 연구함으로써 새로운 통찰을 얻을 수 있다

data.background: 전통적으로 인류학은 타문화 연구를 중심으로 발전했으나, 최근 자문화를 객관적으로 분석하는 연구가 증가하고 있다. Gillian Tett는 이 방법을 적용해 2008 금융위기를 예측한 대표적 사례이다.

In recent years, a number of anthropologists have
decided to flip the lens. Throughout much of the
history of this field, researchers from Western countries
focused on the study of cultures that were beyond their
own experiences. Being an “objective” outsider was
thought to be the best way to draw accurate conclusions
about the society being observed. Now, however, some
researchers have begun to utilize anthropological
techniques to increase their knowledge of the societies
that they themselves are a part of. Noted anthropologist
Gillian Tett decided to apply the practices of this
discipline to better understand corporations — she used
the framework of anthropology to immerse herself in the
banking sector and came to the conclusion that “risks
were building in this strange, shadowy world.” Because
of her willingness to view an aspect of her own culture
with the same level of neutrality that anthropologists
traditionally strive for when studying more “exotic”
ones, Tett was one of the few people to accurately predict

data.topic_en: The Importance of Structure in Business Innovation
data.topic_ko: 기업 혁신에서 직원 자율성만으로는 창의성이 보장되지 않으며, 명확한 목표, 경영진 지원, 진행 측정 시스템 등의 구조가 필수적이다.
data.expected_title: Structure, Not Freedom: The Real Key to Innovation
data.text_type: 논설문 (argumentative) — 통념 반박 후 주장 전개
data.mood_change: 통념 제시 → 반박 → 대안 제시 (논리적 전개)
data.passage_html: There is a common assumption that innovation in business is the result of extensive employee autonomy. Imagine a software company that hopes to create groundbreaking applications that will capture the imagination of consumers. To achieve this goal, it may decide to release several of its best developers from their current projects and allow them to explore different ideas without restraint. Contrary to expectation, granting this freedom will not necessarily stimulate their creativity or produce breakthrough ideas. The key to meaningful innovation is structure — employees need clearly defined goals, extensive support from management, and systems to measure progress. All of this is necessary to ensure that the ideas being generated are practical and fit into the company's business model, as well as to set up an infrastructure to effectively execute any concepts with the potential to develop into successful products.
data.topic_sentence_en: The key to meaningful innovation is structure — employees need clearly defined goals, extensive support from management, and systems to measure progress.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 의미 있는 혁신의 핵심은 구조이다 — 직원들은 명확히 정의된 목표, 경영진의 광범위한 지원, 진행 상황을 측정하는 시스템이 필요하다. 자율성보다 체계적 구조가 혁신을 이끈다는 주장.

data.topic_en: Topic: The difficulty of faking emotions through vocal cues
data.topic_ko: 우리의 목소리와 비언어적 감탄사(vocal bursts)는 최대 24가지 감정을 드러낼 수 있으며, 이를 통해 타인이 우리의 진짜 감정을 파악할 수 있다. 따라서 감정을 가짜로 꾸미기는 매우 어렵다.
data.expected_title: The Revealing Power of Vocal Bursts: Why Emotions Are Hard to Fake
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 연구 결과 제시 / 예시 → 결론 구조
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (설명문)
data.passage_html: Have you ever felt depressed but told a friend 'Everything is great'? Chances are, the sound of your voice — and perhaps a sigh — gave you away and failed to convince your friend of the truthfulness of your statement. According to the results of research published in American Psychologist, up to 24 kinds of emotions are decipherable from the tone of our voice and our nonverbal exclamations, known as vocal bursts. They can reveal complex emotions ranging from surprise (gasp) and realization (ohhh) to interest (ah?) and confusion (huh?). In the study, more than 2,000 vocal bursts were recorded by actors around the world and then evaluated by people recruited online. All of the vocal bursts were then categorized as correlating with a recognizable emotion. This shows that, whether we are aware of our own vocal bursts or not, others pick up on them and can identify them, suggesting that our feelings are difficult to fake.
data.topic_sentence_en: This shows that, whether we are aware of our own vocal bursts or not, others pick up on them and can identify them, suggesting that our feelings are difficult to fake.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 이는 우리가 자신의 vocal bursts를 인지하든 하지 못하든, 다른 사람들이 그것을 포착하고 식별할 수 있음을 보여주며, 우리의 감정을 가짜로 꾸미기 어렵다는 것을 시사한다.

to fake.

data.topic_en: Topic: Copyright Issues with NFTs
data.topic_ko: NFT는 디지털 자산의 소유권을 증명하는 토큰으로 인기를 얻고 있지만, 저작권 보유자가 아닌 사람도 NFT를 발행할 수 있어 저작권 침해 위험이 존재한다.
data.expected_title: NFTs: Ownership Certificates with Copyright Risks
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 개념 정의 후 문제점 제시
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (중립적 설명)
data.passage_html: NFTs, or non-fungible tokens, are digital tokens that are purchased and sold online and represent real-world objects such as photos, videos, and artwork. NFTs function as certificates of ownership for those items, and these tokens cannot be duplicated, meaning that such a certificate is not interchangeable and there can only be one owner at a time. Although many NFTs are costly, buying them is quickly gaining popularity as doing so gives a collector a unique and special connection to the work, with their purchase being marked on a blockchain. However, as there are currently no regulations governing the making and selling of NFTs, there are copyright threats associated with them. This is due to the fact that anyone — not just the copyright holder — can publish a token for a particular item on the blockchain and put that NFT on the market, a process also known as 'minting.' This means there is a possibility artists might see an NFT created for their work without their permission, which is comparable to using copyrighted material in your work without securing a license.
data.topic_sentence_en: This is due to the fact that anyone — not just the copyright holder — can publish a token for a particular item on the blockchain and put that NFT on the market.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 저작권 보유자뿐만 아니라 누구나 블록체인에 특정 항목에 대한 토큰을 게시하고 그 NFT를 시장에 내놓을 수 있다는 사실 때문이며, 이것이 저작권 위협의 핵심 원인이다.

data.passage:
NFTs, or non-fungible tokens, are digital tokens that
are purchased and sold online and represent real-world
objects such as photos, videos, and artwork. NFTs
function as certificates of ownership for those items, and
these tokens cannot be duplicated, meaning that such a
certificate is not interchangeable and there can only be
one owner at a time. Although many NFTs are costly,
buying them is quickly gaining popularity as doing so
gives a collector a unique and special connection to the
work, with their purchase being marked on a blockchain.
However, as there are currently no regulations governing
the making and selling of NFTs, there are copyright
threats associated with them. This is due to the fact that
anyone — not just the copyright holder — can publish a
token for a particular item on the blockchain and put that
NFT on the market, a process also known as “minting.”
This means there is a possibility artists might see an NFT
created for their work without their permission, which is
comparable to using copyrighted material in your work
without securing a license.

data.topic_en: Topic: The Ptolemaic System and its Replacement by the Heliocentric Model
data.topic_ko: 프톨레마이오스 체계는 지구 중심의 우주 모델로 1400년간 지속되었다. 코페르니쿠스가 태양 중심 모델을 제안하며 이를 뒤집었다.
data.expected_title: The Fall of the Geocentric Universe
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 개념 소개 및 역사적 변화 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (객관적 설명)
data.passage_html: The Ptolemaic System, devised by Ptolemy in the second century AD, is a mathematical model in which an immobile Earth is situated at the center of the universe. Other celestial bodies, including the planets, Moon, Sun, and stars, revolved around Earth in this system. According to Ptolemy, the universe is composed of fixed, transparent spheres, and heavenly bodies are attached to these spheres which have their own particular rotations. The celestial sphere, which is the largest, exists to hold the stars and marks the boundary of this universe. This concept of spheres making up the universe with Earth at its center endured long after Ptolemy and wasn't overturned until around 1400 years later. It was then that Copernicus proposed a heliocentric model of the universe, meaning that Earth and the other planets orbit the Sun.
data.topic_sentence_en: This concept of spheres making up the universe with Earth at its center endured long after Ptolemy and wasn't overturned until around 1400 years later.

data.passage: The Ptolemaic System, devised by Ptolemy in the second century AD, is a mathematical model in which an immobile Earth is situated at the center of the universe. Other celestial bodies, including the planets, Moon, Sun, and stars, revolved (또는 revolve) around Earth in this system. According to Ptolemy, the universe is composed of fixed, transparent spheres, and heavenly bodies are attached to these spheres which have their own particular rotations. The celestial sphere, which is the largest, exists to hold the stars and marks the boundary of this universe. This concept of spheres making up the universe with Earth at its center endured long after Ptolemy and wasn’t overturned until around 1400 years later. It was then that Copernicus proposed a heliocentric model of the universe, meaning that Earth and the other planets orbit the Sun.

data.topic_en: Topic: John Cage's experimental music and the concept of silence
data.topic_ko: 작곡가 존 케이지는 전통을 거부하고 실험적인 음악을 추구했다. 그의 대표작 4'33''은 연주자가 아무 소리도 내지 않지만, 청중이 주변의 환경음을 인식하게 하여 침묵 속에서도 '음악'이 존재함을 보여준다.
data.expected_title: John Cage and 4'33'': Music in Silence
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 인물 소개 및 작품 개념 설명 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (설명문)
data.passage_html: John Cage (1912–1992) was a composer known for defying tradition. He experimented with playing instruments in unconventional ways and piecing together tones and rhythms that did not follow established musical structures. He is best known for a controversial piece that, from a traditional perspective, can hardly be called 'music.' That piece is 4'33'', so named because it lasts exactly four minutes and 33 seconds. For its duration, neither instruments nor vocalists produce a single note. The idea for Cage was that, throughout the period of 'silence,' the audience would find that there was not silence at all; they would become aware of the ambient sounds around them that would typically be ignored. Cage had toyed with the idea of the piece for years, but he became convinced of its value after spending time in an anechoic chamber, where the silence was so overwhelming that he was able to hear his own blood circulation.
data.topic_sentence_en: The idea for Cage was that, throughout the period of 'silence,' the audience would find that there was not silence at all; they would become aware of the ambient sounds around them that would typically be ignored.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 케이지의 아이디어는 '침묵'의 시간 동안 청중이 침묵이 전혀 없다는 것을 발견하고, 보통 무시되는 주변의 환경음을 인식하게 된다는 것이다. 이는 작품의 핵심 개념이자 목적을 나타낸다.

grammar_points: 관계대명사 that + 삽입구: 'a piece that, from a traditional perspective, can hardly...' 구조에서 관계대명사절 내 삽입구 위치 파악
neither A nor B 구문: 'neither instruments nor vocalists produce' 부정 병렬에서 동사는 B(vocalists)에 수 일치
동격절 that: 'The idea was that...' 명사 idea의 내용을 설명하는 that절은 접속사
관계대명사 주격 vs 목적격: 'sounds that would typically be ignored'에서 that은 주격

data.background: 존 케이지는 20세기 실험 음악의 선구자로, prepared piano(피아노 내부에 물체를 넣어 소리 변형), 우연성 음악 등을 개척했다. 4'33''은 1952년 초연되었으며, 연주자가 침묵하는 동안 청중의 기침, 의자 소리 등이 '음악'이 되는 개념적 작품이다.

data.passage: John Cage (1912–1992) was a composer known for defying tradition. He experimented with playing instruments in unconventional ways and piecing together tones and rhythms that did not follow established musical structures. He is best known for a controversial piece that, from a traditional perspective, can hardly be called "music." That piece is 4'33", so named because it lasts exactly four minutes and 33 seconds. For its duration, neither instruments nor vocalists produce a single note. The idea for Cage was that, throughout the period of "silence," the audience would find that there was not silence at all; they would become aware of the ambient sounds around them that would typically be ignored. Cage had toyed with the idea of the piece for years, but he became convinced of its value after spending time in an anechoic chamber, where the silence was so overwhelming that he was able to hear his own blood circulation.

data.topic_en: Topic: The Socially Constructed Nature of Beauty and Ugliness in Art
data.topic_ko: 미와 추의 개념은 사회적으로 구성된 것이며, '추한' 예술 작품도 관람자로 하여금 사고하게 하고 새로운 아이디어를 수용하게 만들어 비판적 담론을 촉진할 수 있다는 내용입니다.
data.expected_title: The Value of Ugliness: Redefining Aesthetic Standards in Art
data.text_type: 논설문 (argumentative) — 개념 재정의 및 주장 전개
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (논리적 주장 전개)
data.passage_html: 'Only in relation to our imagination can things be called beautiful or ugly...' Baruch Spinoza points out the socially constructed nature of beauty and ugliness through his theories, as ideas and definitions related to these concepts have shifted so often over time. Artistically speaking, those words have both had moral and aesthetic implications; 'ugly' has denoted the bad while 'beautiful' has been applied to the good. But are these terms this simple? Why must 'ugly' have a negative connotation? After all, ugly art can be interesting and unique. In fact, pieces that we deem ugly can make those who gaze upon them think, and can trigger a receptive state of being to new ideas: something a typically beautiful piece of art could have a more difficult time accomplishing. By using fine art techniques to make something look 'ugly,' an artist can encourage more critical discourse between people as they view the piece. They might question their previous assumptions about beauty and embrace new ideas, which is a key characteristic of compelling art anyway.

data.passage: "Only in relation to our imagination can things be called beautiful or ugly..." Baruch Spinoza points out the socially constructed nature of beauty and ugliness through his theories, as ideas and definitions related to these concepts have shifted so often over time. Artistically speaking, those words have both had moral and aesthetic implications; "ugly" has denoted the bad while "beautiful" has been applied to the good. But are these terms this simple? Why must "ugly" have a negative connotation? After all, ugly art can be interesting and unique. In fact, pieces that we deem ugly can make those who gaze upon them think, and can trigger a receptive state of being to new ideas: something a typically beautiful piece of art could have a more difficult time accomplishing. By using fine art techniques to make something look "ugly," an artist can encourage more critical discourse between people as they view the piece. They might question their previous assumptions about beauty and embrace new ideas, which is a key characteristic of compelling art anyway.

data.topic_en: Topic: Forgotten memories are not lost but archived in the brain
data.topic_ko: 잊혀진 기억도 뇌에 저장되어 있다. fMRI 연구는 학생들이 기억하지 못하는 단어도 원래의 신경 패턴이 여전히 활성화됨을 보여주었다. 기억은 접근하기 어려운 뇌의 영역에 보관되어 있을 뿐이다.
data.expected_title: Archived Memories: What We Forget Is Not Lost
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 연구 결과 제시 / 주장·근거 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (중립적 설명)
data.passage_html: That which is forgotten is not always lost. Research shows that memories remain stored even if we can't readily retrieve them. Take the brain activity of numerous university students who were observed during memory formation and recall. When students were asked to remember as much as they could about a list of words they'd been given earlier in the study, fMRI scans showed that the brain activity throughout recall was the same as in the course of the learning process. Even when students had forgotten most of what they had learned, the original neurological patterns were still picked up, illustrating that the memories were still there despite being unattainable. Much is still unclear regarding how long memories persist, but evidence like this has shown that it might be longer than we think. Therefore, it would not be wrong to say that memories are just archived in the less accessible parts of our brains.
data.topic_sentence_en: Therefore, it would not be wrong to say that memories are just archived in the less accessible parts of our brains.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 따라서 기억은 우리 뇌의 접근하기 어려운 부분에 보관되어 있을 뿐이라고 말하는 것이 틀리지 않을 것이다. 잊혀진 기억도 사라지지 않고 뇌 어딘가에 저장되어 있다는 핵심 주장.

data.background: fMRI(기능적 자기공명영상)는 뇌 활동 시 혈류 변화를 측정하여 특정 영역의 활성화를 관찰하는 기술이다. 신경 패턴(neurological pattern)은 특정 기억이나 경험과 연결된 뇌 신경세포들의 활동 형태를 의미한다.

data.passage: That which is forgotten is not always lost. Research shows that memories remain stored even if we can’t readily retrieve them. Take the brain activity of numerous university students who were observed during memory formation and recall. When students were asked to remember as much as they could about a list of words they’d been given earlier in the study, fMRI scans showed that the brain activity throughout recall was the same as in the course of the learning process. Even when students had forgotten most of what they had learned, the original neurological patterns were still picked up, illustrating that the memories were still there despite being unattainable. Much is still unclear regarding how long memories persist, but evidence like this has shown that it might be longer than we think. Therefore, it would not be wrong to say that memories are just archived in the less accessible parts of our brains.

data.topic_ko: 보조 기술은 장애인의 독립성을 높이지만, 복잡성 증가로 충분한 교육이 필요하다. 지속적 지원을 받는 사람들은 새로운 보조 기술을 효과적으로 활용하여 삶의 질을 크게 향상시킬 수 있다.
data.expected_title: Training and Support: Keys to Assistive Technology Success
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 문제 제시 및 해결책 제안
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (설명·논증 중심 글)
data.passage_html: Assistive technologies allow disabled individuals to perform an array of tasks without the aid of another person, thereby increasing their level of independence. Although recent developments have improved the functionality of assistive technologies, the corresponding increase in complexity necessitates that sufficient training be offered to the people with disabilities who will use them. While some devices can be easily incorporated into a person's life with a minimum of instruction, others are more sophisticated and require a significant amount of knowledge to be utilized effectively. In fact, a number of studies have shown that people who avoid new assistive technologies report feeling intimidated by the complex interfaces and many optional features, which leads them to believe that they are unqualified to operate them. Fortunately, individuals who are provided with access to ongoing support have an increased likelihood of overcoming this barrier and integrating new assistive technologies into their day-to-day routine, greatly enhancing the overall quality of their life.
data.topic_sentence_en: Fortunately, individuals who are provided with access to ongoing support have an increased likelihood of overcoming this barrier and integrating new assistive technologies into their day-to-day routine, greatly enhancing the overall quality of their life.
data.topic_sentence_ko: 다행히, 지속적인 지원을 받는 개인들은 이러한 장벽을 극복하고 새로운 보조 기술을 일상에 통합할 가능성이 높아지며, 이는 삶의 질을 크게 향상시킨다는 핵심 주장.

data.passage: Assistive technologies allow disabled individuals to perform an array of tasks without the aid of another person, thereby increasing their level of independence. Although recent developments have improved the functionality of assistive technologies, the corresponding increase in complexity necessitates that sufficient training be offered to the people with disabilities who will use them. While some devices can be easily incorporated into a person’s life with a minimum of instruction, others are more sophisticated and require a significant amount of knowledge to be utilized effectively. In fact, a number of studies have shown that people who avoid new assistive technologies report feeling intimidated by the complex interfaces and many optional features, which leads them to believe that they are unqualified to operate them. Fortunately, individuals who are provided with access to ongoing support have an increased likelihood of overcoming this barrier and integrating new assistive technologies into their day-to-day routine, greatly enhancing the overall quality of their life.

data.topic_en: Topic: Social norms and moral behavior shaped by the 'impartial spectator'
data.topic_ko: Adam Smith는 사람들이 자기 이익을 추구하지만, 사회 구성원으로서 타인의 기대에 따라 행동하며, 내면의 '공정한 관찰자'(양심)가 도덕적 행동을 유도한다고 주장했다.
data.expected_title: The Impartial Spectator: Smith's Theory of Moral Behavior
data.text_type: 논설문 (expository) — 철학적 이론 설명 / 개념 전개 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (논리 전개형)
data.passage_html: In Adam Smith's 1759 work The Theory of Moral Sentiments, he posited that, although everyone is driven to a certain extent by self-interest and personal passions, we act in accordance with others' expectations. As members of society, we are keenly aware of what behaviors are considered objectionable to the majority and what are not based on the reactions we receive or witness. Because we have a natural ability to empathize and want others to see us as morally accountable, we are taught that we must do what is perceived as the right thing. To help us determine whether we are doing the right thing, Smith suggested that we each have a conscience that informs us of how our behavior will be interpreted. This 'impartial spectator,' as he called it, prevents us from being overly interested in our own affairs and causes us to do things that are worthy of society's praise rather than its reproach.
data.topic_sentence_en: This 'impartial spectator,' as he called it, prevents us from being overly interested in our own affairs and causes us to do things that are worthy of society's praise rather than its reproach.
data.topic_sentence_ko: Smith가 명명한 '공정한 관찰자'(양심)는 우리가 자기 일에만 과도하게 관심을 갖지 못하게 하고, 사회의 비난보다는 칭찬받을 만한 일을 하도록 만든다는 핵심 주장.

transformations.mood_change_desc:
이 지문은 심경 변화가 아닌 논리 전개형으로, Adam Smith의 이론을 단계적으로 설명한다.
① 자기 이익 vs 사회 기대 대조 → ② 사회적 인식 근거 → ③ 공감과 도덕성 욕구 → ④ 양심의 역할 제시 → ⑤ 공정한 관찰자의 기능(결론).
grammar_points: 양보절 'although + 주어 + 동사'와 주절의 대조 구조
수동태 'are considered objectionable': ~로 여겨지다
'want + 목적어 + to부정사': 5형식 사역 유사 구조
'prevent A from B' / 'cause A to do' 병렬 구조
transformations.title_theme_options:
제목: '양심이 만드는 도덕적 행동' 또는 '공정한 관찰자의 역할'
주제: 사람들은 자기 이익을 추구하지만, 내면의 양심('공정한 관찰자')이 사회적 기대에 부합하는 도덕적 행동을 유도한다.

data.background: Adam Smith의 '도덕감정론'(1759)은 경제학의 '국부론'보다 먼저 쓰인 윤리학 저서로, impartial spectator(공정한 관찰자) 개념을 통해 인간의 도덕적 판단과 사회적 행동을 설명한다. 이는 후대 사회심리학의 '내면화된 규범' 이론과 연결된다.

data.passage: In Adam Smith’s 1759 work The Theory of Moral Sentiments, he posited that, although everyone is driven to a certain extent by self-interest and personal passions, we act in accordance with others’ expectations. As members of society, we are keenly aware of what behaviors are considered objectionable to the majority and what are not based on the reactions we receive or witness. Because we have a natural ability to empathize and want others to see us as morally accountable, we are taught that we must do what is perceived as the right thing. To help us determine whether we are doing the right thing, Smith suggested that we each have a conscience that informs us of how our behavior will be interpreted. This “impartial spectator,” as he called it, prevents us from being overly interested in our own affairs and causes us to do things that are worthy of society’s praise rather than its reproach.

data.topic_en: Topic: Hidden Behavioral Expressions of Suppressed Anger
data.topic_ko: 숨겨진 분노는 무의식적 신체 행동(안면 경련, 이갈이 등)과 의도적이지만 간접적인 행동(지각, 지나친 공손함 등)으로 나타난다. 이러한 행동들은 흔히 무해한 성격 결함으로 여겨지며 분노와 연결되지 않는다.
data.expected_title: Unrecognized Behavioral Manifestations of Hidden Anger
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 분류·예시 중심 / 현상 분석
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (객관적 설명)
data.passage_html: In a study on hidden anger, researchers observed that certain unintended behaviors often come forward. One group of them consists of repetitive physical acts, such as facial tics and spasmodic foot movements. They may also include uncontrollable behaviors such as poor sleeping habits, jaw clenching, and teeth grinding, which others may notice but not associate with anger. Other behaviors are deliberate but not directly linked to anger, and these may be expressed as procrastination, habitual lateness, excessive politeness, or inappropriate sarcasm and flippancy in conversation. Whatever the case, these behaviors are often attributed to harmless personality flaws or eccentricities. Few if any people ever associate them with deeper issues that might have been provoked by anger.
data.topic_sentence_en: Whatever the case, these behaviors are often attributed to harmless personality flaws or eccentricities.

data.passage: In a study on hidden anger, researchers observed that certain unintended behaviors often come forward. One group of them consists of repetitive physical acts, such as facial tics and spasmodic foot movements. They may also include uncontrollable behaviors such as poor sleeping habits, jaw clenching, and teeth grinding, which others may notice but not associate with anger. Other behaviors are deliberate but not directly linked to anger, and these may be expressed as procrastination, habitual lateness, excessive politeness, or inappropriate sarcasm and flippancy in conversation. Whatever the case, these behaviors are often attributed to harmless personality flaws or eccentricities. Few if any people ever associate them with deeper issues that might have been provoked by anger.

data.topic_en: Topic: The loss of art's aura in the age of mechanical reproduction
data.topic_ko: 발터 벤야민은 기계적 복제 시대에 예술 작품의 아우라(고유한 정신)가 사라졌다고 주장했다. 복제 기술은 작품을 원본 맥락에서 분리시켜 개인적 경험을 제거하고, 대중이 집단적으로 예술을 흡수하게 만들었다.
data.expected_title: The Destruction of Art's Aura in the Age of Mechanical Reproduction
data.text_type: 논설문 (argumentative) — 주장 제시 후 논리 전개
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (철학적 논증)
data.passage_html:
In his 1935 essay 'The Work of Art in the Age of Mechanical Reproduction,' Walter Benjamin contended that the aura of original art had disappeared in the modern age as a result of new forms of replicable media technology.

(B) For Benjamin, art possessed an aura that was derived from its physical uniqueness. Making a copy, as one might do with a photograph or a film, he argued, removed the artwork from its original context, destroying its authenticity.

(A) The destruction of the vital force that gives art its individuality — its spirit — altered the effect of art on viewers. Before the age of mechanical reproduction, viewing an image or an object required one to be in the presence of the original piece, allowing for a more personal experience.

(C) By producing copies and making it possible for many people to see the work simultaneously, however, a viewer could no longer be fully absorbed in it because the individual connection was eliminated. Instead, the viewers, 'the distracted mass,' collectively absorbed the art.

data.passage:
In his 1935 essay “The Work of Art in the Age of Mechanical Reproduction,” Walter Benjamin contended that the aura of original art had disappeared in the modern age as a result of new forms of replicable media technology.

(B) For Benjamin, art possessed an aura that was derived from its physical uniqueness. Making a copy, as one might do with a photograph or a film, he argued, removed the artwork from its original context, destroying its authenticity.

(A) The destruction of the vital force that gives art its individuality — its spirit — altered the effect of art on viewers. Before the age of mechanical reproduction, viewing an image or an object required one to be in the presence of the original piece, allowing for a more personal experience.

(C) By producing copies and making it possible for many people to see the work simultaneously, however, a viewer could no longer be fully absorbed in it because the individual connection was eliminated. Instead, the viewers, “the distracted mass,” collectively absorbed the art.

data.topic_en: Topic: The Principle of Methodological Naturalism in Science
data.topic_ko: 과학적 방법론은 방법론적 자연주의 원리에 기반한다. 이는 자연적 사건과 현상 연구를 통해서만 진리를 깨달을 수 있으며, 실험 가능한 물리적 대상만을 다루고 믿음이나 감정을 배제하는 계몽주의 이후 과학의 핵심 요건이다.
data.expected_title: Methodological Naturalism: The Foundation of Scientific Method
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 개념 정의 및 논리적 전개
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (객관적 설명)
data.passage_html:
The scientific method is based on the principle of methodological naturalism.

(C) Proponents of this approach to providing explanations about the universe assert that truths can only be realized by studying natural events and phenomena. In particular, they prefer that the objects of scientific research be tangible so that it is absolutely clear that they are real and not imagined.

(A) The philosophy behind this strategy is that natural things in the physical world can be tested and experimented on, whereas theories about other potential causes for events cannot. Essentially, methodological naturalism embraces the skepticism of the phrase 'seeing is believing.'

(B) In other words, it does not allow for belief or emotion to be considered. This requirement has formed an essential part of the scientific method since the Enlightenment, when logic and reason began to replace old worldviews.
data.topic_sentence_en: The scientific method is based on the principle of methodological naturalism.

data.passage:
The scientific method is based on the principle of methodological naturalism.

(C) Proponents of this approach to providing explanations about the universe assert that truths can only be realized by studying natural events and phenomena. In particular, they prefer that the objects of scientific research be tangible so that it is absolutely clear that they are real and not imagined.

(A) The philosophy behind this strategy is that natural things in the physical world can be tested and experimented on, whereas theories about other potential causes for events cannot. Essentially, methodological naturalism embraces the skepticism of the phrase “seeing is believing.”

(B) In other words, it does not allow for belief or emotion to be considered. This requirement has formed an essential part of the scientific method since the Enlightenment, when logic and reason began to replace old worldviews.

data.topic_en: Topic: Millennials' Declining Television Consumption and the Rise of Online Streaming
data.topic_ko: 밀레니얼 세대는 65세 이상 세대에 비해 TV 시청 시간이 절반 수준이며, 지난 20년간 더욱 감소했다. 인터넷과 온라인 스트리밍을 선호하는 경향이 주요 원인이지만, 스트리밍 형태로 TV 매체를 완전히 버리지는 않았다.
data.expected_title: Millennials and the Shift from Traditional Television to Online Streaming
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 통계 제시 및 세대 간 비교·원인 분석 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (객관적 사실 전달)
data.passage_html: According to statistics, millennials watch far less television than older generations. The average American over 65 watches almost four and a half hours of television a day; the average millennial, meanwhile, watches just two. Furthermore, the amount of television millennials watch has declined in the last two decades, while older generations are watching more. Several factors may explain this disparity, the most prominent of which is the Internet. While older generations still rely on television for most of their entertainment and news, millennials prefer to consume both online. A recent study has shown that 9 percent more millennials stream online videos than watch television, a trend that shows no signs of slowing. Still, a relatively new technological trend could be bringing millennials back to television, but via other media. As a form of television watching, streaming differs considerably from the analogue viewing style of older generations, but it nonetheless reveals that this younger generation hasn't entirely abandoned the medium.

data.topic_sentence_ko: TV 시청의 한 형태로서 스트리밍은 기성세대의 아날로그 시청 방식과 상당히 다르지만, 그럼에도 젊은 세대가 TV 매체를 완전히 버리지는 않았음을 보여준다. 시청 방식의 변화일 뿐 매체 자체의 포기는 아니라는 핵심을 전달한다.

data.topic_en: Topic: The First Scientific Description of Colorblindness
data.topic_ko: 1794년 존 달튼이 최초로 색맹, 특히 적록색맹에 대한 과학적 설명을 발표했다. 이는 망막의 원추세포 이상으로 발생하며, 유전적이거나 후천적 요인에 의해 나타난다.
data.expected_title: John Dalton and the Discovery of Colorblindness
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 과학적 발견과 원리 설명 중심
data.mood_change: 중립적 설명 (객관적 과학 서술)
data.passage_html: In 1794, the English chemist and physicist John Dalton published an academic article that included the first description of colorblindness. In it, he provided a summary of his research regarding red-green colorblindness, which is now known to be caused by a problem with the light-sensitive cells called cones that are found in the retina of the human eye. Typically, a person has three sets of fully functional cones — each responds to different light wavelengths, enabling the eye to perceive the three primary colors. However, in some cases, the cells that detect either red or green are less sensitive than normal or are missing altogether. Regardless of which of these types of cones are deficient, the end result for the affected individual is the same. It becomes difficult or even impossible to distinguish between these two colors. This form of colorblindness is usually genetic, but it can also result from physical damage to the eye or even old age.
