data.passage: In 1794, the English chemist and physicist John Dalton published an academic article that included the first description of colorblindness. In it, he provided a summary of his research regarding red-green colorblindness, which is now known to be caused by a problem with the light-sensitive cells called cones that are found in the retina of the human eye. Typically, a person has three sets of fully functional cones — each responds to different light wavelengths, enabling the eye to perceive the three primary colors. However, in some cases, the cells that detect either red or green are less sensitive than normal or are missing altogether. Regardless of which of these types of cones are deficient, the end result for the affected individual is the same. It becomes difficult or even impossible to distinguish between these two colors. This form of colorblindness is usually genetic, but it can also result from physical damage to the eye or even old age.

data.topic_en: Topic: The effectiveness of peer influence in reducing school bullying
data.topic_ko: 프린스턴 대학 연구팀이 학교 폭력 감소를 위해 또래 영향력을 활용한 실험을 진행했다. 사회적 영향력이 있는 학생들이 반폭력 메시지를 전파하자 학교 내 폭력이 감소하고 외교적 해결이 증가했다.
data.expected_title: Peer Modeling as an Effective Anti-Bullying Strategy
data.text_type: 설명문 (expository) — 연구 과정과 결과 중심
data.mood_change: 해당 없음 (연구 보고 형식)
data.passage_html: In search of a new, more effective strategy to reduce bullying at schools, Princeton University psychologist Betsy Levy Paluck and her research team turned to students themselves or, more specifically, students who were identified by their classmates as trendsetters, team leaders, and peer role models — known in scientific terms as 'social referents.' According to social psychology theory, individuals learn what is desirable by observing other people's behavior and seeing what is rewarded or punished. Social referents, Paluck said, might play a significant role in shaping what is considered acceptable behavior. With that in mind, Paluck and her team worked with schools across New Jersey in the 2012–2013 school year and staged bullying intervention programs at half of them. In these intervention programs, student social referents — students with the most connections to other students — were coached to spread anti-conflict messages. At the end of the year, it was found that schools where the intervention programs had taken place reported less violence and more diplomacy among students, demonstrating how powerful peer modeling can be.

sentences.sentence_html: In search of a new, more effective strategy to reduce bullying at schools, Princeton University psychologist Betsy Levy Paluck and her research team turned to students themselves or, more specifically, students who were identified by their classmates as trendsetters, team leaders, and peer role models — known in scientific terms as 'social referents.'

sentences.sentence_html: According to social psychology theory, individuals learn what is desirable by observing other people's behavior and seeing what is rewarded or punished.
sentences.translation: 사회 심리학 이론에 따르면, / 개인들은 무엇이 바람직한지를 배운다 / 다른 사람들의 행동을 관찰하고 / 무엇이 보상받거나 처벌받는지를 / 보는 것을 통해.

sentences.sentence_html: Social referents, Paluck said, might play a significant role in shaping what is considered acceptable behavior.

sentences.sentence_html: In these intervention programs, student social referents — students with the most connections to other students — were coached to spread anti-conflict messages.

sentences.sentence_html: At the end of the year, it was found that schools where the intervention programs had taken place reported less violence and more diplomacy among students, demonstrating how powerful peer modeling can be.

blank_inference: 빈칸: 또래 모델링이 얼마나 _____ 수 있는지 → 'powerful' (강력한)
빈칸: 사회적 준거자들이 _____ 행동을 형성하는 데 역할 → 'acceptable' (수용 가능한)
빈칸: 학생들에게 _____메시지를 전파하도록 지도 → 'anti-conflict' (반갈등)
transformations.mood_change_desc:
이 지문은 연구 보고 형식으로 심경 변화가 없으며,
연구 배경 → 이론적 근거 → 가설 → 실험 실행 → 결과 도출의
논리적 단계를 따른다.
grammar_points: 관계대명사 who: 'students who were identified'는 주격 관계대명사절로 선행사 students 수식
의문사절 what: 'what is desirable', 'what is considered'는 명사절로 동사의 목적어 역할
조동사 might: 'might play'는 가능성·추측을 나타내며 과거 시점의 예측 표현
분사구문 demonstrating: 결과를 나타내는 현재분사로, 주절의 결과를 부연 설명
transformations.title_theme_options:
제목: '또래 영향력을 통한 학교 폭력 감소' 또는 '사회적 준거자의 역할'
주제: 또래 모델링의 효과 / 학생 주도 반폭력 프로그램의 성공

data.background: 사회 심리학에서 사회적 준거자(social referents)는 집단 내에서 영향력이 큰 개인으로, 다른 사람들이 행동 규범을 학습하는 데 중요한 역할을 한다. 프린스턴 대학 연구팀은 이러한 또래 영향력을 활용하여 학교 폭력 감소 실험을 진행했다.

data.passage: In search of a new, more effective strategy to reduce bullying at schools, Princeton University psychologist Betsy Levy Paluck and her research team turned to students themselves or, more specifically, students who were identified by their classmates as trendsetters, team leaders, and peer role models — known in scientific terms as “social referents.” According to social psychology theory, individuals learn what is desirable by observing other people’s behavior and seeing what is rewarded or punished. Social referents, Paluck said, might play a significant role in shaping what is considered acceptable behavior. With that in mind, Paluck and her team worked with schools across New Jersey in the 2012–2013 school year and staged bullying intervention programs at half of them. In these intervention programs, student social referents — students with the most connections to other students — were coached to spread anti-conflict messages. At the end of the year, it was found that schools where the intervention programs had taken place reported less violence and more diplomacy among students, demonstrating how powerful peer modeling can be.
